description: Official Lados quantity surveying and commercial management capability pack.

required: claim evidence rules for claim templates

guardrails: QS nodes produce measurements, valuations, checks, and recommendations.
QS nodes do not certify payment, approve entitlement, or provide legal advice.
Claim, variation, and final account outputs must preserve assumptions, source references, and review status.

verification.manifest: verified — S6 fix: nodes.json only defined 3 of the 7 nodes manifest.json declared (normalize_boq, split_work_packages, value_variation, reconcile_final_account were missing); added all 4. 5 further manifest capabilities (qs.boq.generate_draft, qs.claim.submit, qs.claim.record_certification, qs.variation.submit, qs.variation.record_approval) have no corresponding node in manifest.json's own `nodes` list either — deferred as a known gap, same class as S2's workflow-foundation deferral, not blocking since all 7 *declared nodes* are implemented
verification.runtime: implemented — all 7 nodes implemented. classify_trade/value_variation/reconcile_final_account/assess_progress_claim are deterministic rules-based advisory computations, not real AI/LLM calls. No new resource types needed: reuses boq/progress_claim/variation from migration 0041. split_work_packages and reconcile_final_account are deliberately stateless (no resource persisted) — human acceptance happens via a separate lados.human.request_approval node in the composing template
